title: JwksResponse
description: The JWKS payload served at GET /.well-known/jwks.json. Contains every active + retired-but-not-yet-expired signing key. Downstream verifiers cache aggressively per Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600, immutable.

keys.description: Array of signing-key entries. Rotation windows produce two entries (the previous key + the new key) for auth.jwt.rotation_grace_period_days.

kty.description: Key type. `oct` for symmetric-key algorithms (HS256). Wave 1c will add `RSA` (RS256) and `EC` (ES256).

kid.description: Key ID. Verifiers match against the JWT header's `kid`.

k.description: Base64url-encoded shared secret. SAFE on a public endpoint ONLY when identity-service + verifiers communicate over a private network (design.md §8.3 caveat). Wave 1c upgrades to asymmetric keys so this field carries only the public component.

use.description: Key usage. Always `sig` for our use case.

x-http.cache_notes: The Cache-Control is aggressive because rotation windows are longer than the cache TTL. Verifiers that see a JWT with a kid they don't recognise refresh the JWKS on demand (cache-busting via a query string or a soft revalidation).
